title: Not Any Higher Stands The Grave
content:
Not any higher stands the grave
    For heroes than for men;
    Not any nearer for the child
    Than numb three-score and ten.

    This latest leisure equal lulls
    The beggar and his queen;
    Propitiate this democrat
    By summer's gracious mien.
meaning:
Exploring the themes of classic, Emily Elizabeth Dickinson delivers a powerful performance in "Not Any Higher Stands The Grave"...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
